creator: Alexander Terp
description:
Comparing large integers to their float representation gives incorrect results because the int is converted to float, losing precision.

**Repro:**
```
x = 9223372036854775807
print(x == 9223372036854775807.0)  // true (WRONG)
// The float is actually 9223372036854776000 (rounded up)
```

Edge case only relevant at int64 bounds.

**Test files:** `bugs/operators/86_max_int_comparison_bug.rad`, `70_int_float_mixed_compare.rad`
title: Large int vs float comparison gives wrong result due to precision loss
